reasoning: A YES resolution requires Nord Stream 2 AG to be completely removed from the OFAC SDN list by July 1, 2025, which gives a window of less than two months. While there were reports in late April 2025 that the White House (likely envoy Steve Witkoff) was debating lifting these sanctions as part of a peace deal with Russia, prominent Trump administration officials (including Witkoff and Secretary of State Marco Rubio) issued a joint statement forcefully denying that any such discussions had taken place. Even if the administration pursues this policy, moving from preliminary debates/denials to a finalized peace deal and then formally delisting an entity from the SDN list takes a sluggish administrative process. Oftentimes, general licenses are issued first rather than a full initial removal from the SDN list. Moreover, Congress has historically strongly supported sanctions on Nord Stream 2 (e.g. via PEESA), so lifting them could prompt political pushback and hurdles. Given the short timeline (under two months), the official denial of the policy change, and the bureaucratic friction of expedited OFAC delisting, the chances of the entity not appearing on the tool by July 1 are exceedingly low. P is set to the minimal allowed value.
